hero.question: Will the DHS shutdown end after March 31, 2026?
hero.prob_take: The crowd has basically priced in a shutdown resolution at 90.5%, treating a March 31 deadline breach as a tail risk rather than base case. The real question is whether Congress actually passes a budget or just kicks it past Easter again, and at these odds you're getting paid almost nothing for the execution risk.

daily_take.headline: DHS Shutdown Odds Spike to 90%: Market's Pricing in a Done Deal Too Fast
daily_take.deck: The DHS shutdown resolution market jumped 10 points overnight to 90.5%, signaling confidence that Congress will hammer out a deal before the March 31 deadline. But this is peak consensus pricing in a Congress that specializes in brinkmanship. Smart money fades the overheated odds and waits for March turbulence to push resolution probability back to reality (75-80%), then takes the YES at better prices.
